completed_tasks.name: Write tracer bullet: DATA instruction test
Implement CPU (cpu/cpu.go)
completed_tasks.progress: Full CPU implementation written; tracer bullet TestDATALoadsImmediateIntoRegister passes

remaining_tasks.name: Add remaining tests: LOAD, STORE, ADD, JMP, JMPZ, CLF, IAR increment

decisions.decision: Shift all stepper indices +1 (modulo 6) vs reference implementation
decisions.rationale: Our Stepper (components/stepper.go) bootstraps step 0 active in NewStepper() by calling step() with clockIn=false. The reference Stepper starts with all bits false and the first Update(true) triggers a sentinel→reset cycle that makes step 0 active AFTER Update. Net effect: Step() call 1 in our CPU sees stepper[1], but the reference sees stepper[0]. Mapping: ref[0]→ours[1], ref[1]→ours[2], ref[2]→ours[3], ref[3]→ours[4], ref[4]→ours[5], ref[5]→ours[0].

decisions.decision: Use alias sio for simple-computer/io package
decisions.rationale: Avoids collision with Go stdlib io package identifier at the package name level.

next_action: Add LOAD test in cpu_test.go, run go test ./cpu/... to confirm GREEN, then add STORE, ADD, JMP, JMPZ, CLF, and IAR increment tests one at a time. All follow the same pattern as the reference test file at simple-computer/simple-computer/cpu/cpu_test.go.
context_notes: The critical discovery this session was the stepper index offset. The reference CPU.go maps stepper[0] to the first fetch step (IAR->MAR), but our Stepper initializes differently so Step() 1 lands on stepper[1]. All 6 stepper indices needed +1 shift (wrapping 5->0). The fix is already applied in cpu/cpu.go and verified by the passing DATA test.
